summary: We recommend the branch protection now active on outcomes-mcp main because the PUT to the branches main protection endpoint returned a body with required_status_checks contexts equal to outcome-gate and typecheck. The tradeoff is that any future fix to a broken gate must temporarily flip enforce_admins to false then restore it which is documented as the break-glass procedure. The orchestrator can now rely on the gate being non-bypassable for every subsequent tick. The runroc file outcomes runrocs A1 protection json captures the full response body for audit.

claims.statement: branch protection PUT against outcomes-mcp main returned 200 with required_status_checks contexts containing outcome-gate and typecheck because the runroc body shows those two strings under required_status_checks contexts and checks lists.

citations.title: A1 protection runroc captured from gh api PUT response
GitHub REST API branch protection reference

claims.caveats: The two required checks must run on every PR for the protection to be meaningful; both outcome-gate.yml and typecheck.yml are already on main per the earlier orchestrator-bootstrap PR.

claims.statement: enforce_admins is enabled so org admins including alex-jadecli cannot bypass the gate because the runroc body shows enforce_admins enabled equals true.

citations.title: A1 protection runroc enforce_admins enabled field
QUEUE.md operating rules forbid disabling the gate to bypass

claims.caveats: Break-glass procedure for fixing a broken gate is to PATCH enforce_admins to false then restore it; this is acceptable as a documented one-shot not a standing posture.

claims.statement: required_linear_history is enabled so the merge log stays bisectable for future verdicts because the runroc body shows required_linear_history enabled equals true and the repo uses squash merges by repo settings.

citations.title: A1 protection runroc required_linear_history enabled field

claims.caveats: Linear history forbids merge commits; this is fine for this repo because the canonical merge style is squash.

open_questions: Should the orchestrator add a periodic drill outcome that attempts merge bypass and confirms enforce_admins still blocks it?
methodology_notes: Verified by inspecting outcomes runrocs A1 protection json after the PUT. The runroc file was committed alongside the queue update so the orchestrator memory of A1 being done has a tamper-evident on-disk artifact.
